message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "PH-XII-U7-C1",
      "title": "Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",
      "description": "Understanding the particle nature of light, photoelectric effect, wave-particle duality, de Broglie hypothesis, electron diffraction, Davisson-Germer experiment, Compton scattering, X-ray production, and applications in modern physics.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain experimental evidence for the dual nature of radiation (photoelectric effect) and matter (electron diffraction).",
        "Derive and apply Einstein's photoelectric equation to calculate stopping potential and kinetic energy of emitted electrons.",
        "Analyze Compton scattering phenomena and explain wave-particle duality using de Broglie wavelength concepts."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "PH-XII-U8-C1",
      "title": "Atoms",
      "description": "Exploring atomic structure through Rutherford's scattering experiment, Bohr's model postulates, hydrogen spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen), energy levels, and limitations of the classical models.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and deduce the nuclear model of the atom.",
        "Apply Bohr's quantization conditions to calculate orbital radii, velocities, and energies for hydrogen-like atoms.",
        "Explain the origin of atomic spectra using energy level transitions and derive wavelengths for various spectral series."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-VIII Atoms and Nuclei",
      "chapter_name": "Atoms"
    },
    {
      "code": "PH-XII-U8-C2",
      "title": "Nuclei",
      "description": "Investigating nuclear composition, mass defect, binding energy per nucleon curve, radioactive decay laws (alpha, beta, gamma), half-life calculations, and applications in fission and fusion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate mass defect and binding energy to explain nuclear stability using the binding energy per nucleon graph.",
        "Apply the law of radioactive decay to determine activity, mean life, and half-life for given isotopes.",
        "Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes and analyze their energy release mechanisms."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-VIII Atoms and Nuclei",
      "chapter_name": "Nuclei"
    },
    {
      "code": "PH-XII-U9-C1",
      "title": "Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits",
      "description": "Studying semiconductor physics including energy bands, intrinsic vs extrinsic semiconductors (n-type/p-type), p-n junction diode characteristics, rectifiers, Zener diodes as voltage regulators, transistors in common emitter configuration, logic gates, and basic circuit applications.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory and doping effects.",
        "Analyze the V-I characteristics of p-n junction diodes to explain rectification processes in half-wave and full-wave circuits.",
        "Design simple logic gate combinations using NAND/NOR gates and analyze transistor operation as a switch or amplifier."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–IX Electronic Devices",
      "chapter_name": "Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits"
    }
  ]
}
